findings.justification: The download_export_file handler (registered via admin_init) only verifies a nonce and never calls current_user_can(), so any logged-in user who obtains a valid 'product-csv' nonce can trigger a file download without a capability check.

The user-controlled $_GET['filename'] is passed to set_filename() through only wp_unslash() with no sanitization of path-traversal sequences (e.g. '../'), potentially allowing an attacker to read arbitrary previously generated export files from the server.

The user-controlled $_POST['filename'] is passed to set_filename() via wp_unslash() with no sanitization of path-traversal sequences, allowing an authenticated user with export capability to influence the server-side file path used for the generated export.

The $_POST['columns'] and $_POST['selected_columns'] arrays are passed to the exporter via only wp_unslash() with no type or content sanitization, relying entirely on downstream exporter code to safely handle arbitrary user-supplied column names.
